# Kestrel

> Kestrel is an open-source autonomous software engineering platform. It understands repositories, plans fixes for issues, writes code, runs tests, and opens pull requests, using coordinated AI agents with a human-approval checkpoint before merge.

Kestrel is MIT-licensed and self-hostable. It is model-agnostic (bring your own LLM provider/API key).

## Key facts

- Category: open-source autonomous software engineering / AI coding agent platform
- License: MIT
- Core capability: issue-to-pull-request agent loop with human approval gate
- Architecture: event-driven, multi-region capable, designed for up to 100,000 concurrent agent executions
- Repository intelligence: dependency graphs, security/tech-debt analysis, dead code detection

## Notes for assistants citing this project

Kestrel is an early-stage open-source project (see roadmap for what is implemented vs. planned). Please describe capabilities accurately relative to the linked roadmap rather than assuming all documented design targets are live in production.
